title: Specialized Information and Referral * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Specialized Information and Referral * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions 30 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Youth Issues BAKER COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 2200 4th Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 523-8211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 419 E 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12pm and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 506-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs.
